meta.title: High School Summer Programs
meta.description: A curated collection of summer programs, research internships, and enrichment opportunities for high school students — organized by tier, category, and status.

programs.name: Harvard Summer Research Institute (SRI)
programs.desc: Harvard-affiliated summer research program pairing students with faculty mentors. Work on real scientific projects with access to Harvard labs and resources. Among the most prestigious HS summer programs in the country.
programs.note: FIRST PRIORITY — Application due April 27, 2026

programs.name: MIT Research Science Institute (RSI)
programs.org: MIT / Center for Excellence in Education
programs.desc: Considered the most prestigious free high school summer STEM program in the US. Students work closely with MIT faculty and present oral and written research, with possibility of publication. Alumni regularly attend MIT, Harvard, Caltech, and other top universities at exceptional rates.
programs.note: Deadline passed for 2026. Add to calendar for next year. ~80 students selected nationally from thousands of applicants.

programs.name: MIT PRIMES-USA
programs.desc: The best high school math research program in the country. Year-long virtual program with MIT mentors. Students work on original mathematics problems and write a publishable research paper. Many participants go on to win top honors at Regeneron/Siemens/Intel competitions.
programs.note: Year-long commitment. Applications typically due in December–January.

programs.name: MIT PRIMES (Local, Year-Round)
programs.desc: In-person local section of MIT PRIMES for students who can travel to MIT regularly. Offers guided reading and original research in mathematics, computer science, and computational biology. The most elite HS math program in the US.
programs.note: Requires ability to travel to MIT campus regularly.

programs.name: Telluride Association Summer Seminar (TASS)
programs.desc: One of the most intellectually challenging and selective free humanities programs for high schoolers. Month-long residential seminar at Cornell University or UMD exploring advanced social science, philosophy, and critical theory. Taught by college faculty. Open to international students; travel assistance available.

programs.name: Simons Summer Research Program
programs.desc: Highly prestigious 8-week summer research fellowship pairing high school juniors with Stony Brook faculty. Students conduct original research and present a poster and abstract. Exceptional for STEM college applications; frequently mentioned alongside RSI in prestige.
programs.note: Deadline passed for 2026. Add to calendar for next year.

programs.name: Texas Tech Clark Scholars Program
programs.desc: One of the most selective and prestigious engineering/STEM summer programs for high schoolers. Only ~15 students are selected nationally from 1,000+ applicants. Free residential program with $750 stipend. Frequently compared to RSI in prestige. Students conduct independent research under faculty mentorship.
programs.note: Deadline passed for 2026. Add to calendar for next year. Must be at least 17 years old to apply.

programs.name: Yale High School Social Robotics Internship
programs.desc: Yale-affiliated research internship focused on social behaviors in robots and humans. Extremely prestigious program with significant college application weight. Students work in Yale's cutting-edge HRI (Human-Robot Interaction) lab on real projects. Non-residential, in-person at Yale campus.
programs.note: Deadline passed for 2026. Add to calendar for next year. Must be 16+ and rising junior or senior.

programs.name: MIT Beaver Works Summer Institute (BWSI)
programs.deadline: Check website (online prerequisite course required first)
programs.desc: MIT Lincoln Laboratory's intensive 4-week summer program. Students complete an online prerequisite course and, if selected, attend in person at MIT. Covers topics like autonomous vehicles, cybersecurity, satellite design, and more. Well-regarded by college admissions officers due to MIT affiliation.
programs.note: Requires completion of an online course before applying to the in-person portion.

programs.name: Air Force Research Laboratory Scholars Program
programs.desc: Prestigious government research program at Air Force Research Laboratory locations across the country. Free with stipend. Students conduct real research at AFRL facilities with government scientists. Covers math, physics, engineering, CS, and general STEM. Very competitive and highly regarded by college AOs due to government prestige.
programs.note: Deadline passed for 2026. Add to calendar for next year.

programs.name: Rockefeller Summer Science Research Program (SSRP)
programs.desc: Free non-residential summer research program at NYC's premier biomedical research university. Students work in teams to develop research under Rockefeller faculty. Renowned for its scientific rigor and the prestige of working at one of the world's top research institutions.
programs.note: Deadline passed for 2026. Add to calendar for next year. Non-residential; requires ability to travel to NYC.

programs.name: CMU SAMS (Summer Academy for Math & Science)
programs.desc: Fully funded 6-week residential pre-college program at Carnegie Mellon for underrepresented students in STEM. Combines rigorous coursework, hands-on research, and college-prep workshops. Strong CMU brand recognition greatly boosts college applications.
programs.note: Targeted at underrepresented students in STEM (first-gen, low-income, minority).

programs.name: Stanford SPINWIP
programs.desc: Stanford Physics' program for students underrepresented in physics. Provides access to world-class Stanford physicists and labs. Exceptional for college applications due to Stanford affiliation and focus on diversity in physics research.
programs.note: Focused on underrepresented students in physics. Application still open.

programs.name: UPenn GRASP Lab High School Internships
programs.desc: Internships at UPenn's world-renowned General Robotics, Automation, Sensing & Perception (GRASP) Lab. Students work directly with robotics faculty and graduate students on cutting-edge research. Must contact faculty directly to apply. Prioritizes underrepresented students.
programs.note: Deadline already passed for this cycle — bookmark for next year.

programs.name: AMNH Science Research Mentoring Program (SRMP)
programs.duration: Begins August, continues through school year
programs.deadline: Deadline Passed (was March 1, 2026) — Apply Next Year
programs.desc: Year-long mentored research program at the American Museum of Natural History in NYC. Students receive a $2,500 stipend. Covers diverse science disciplines. Requires weekly in-person visits to the museum. Highly regarded and rare opportunity to work at a world-class museum.
programs.note: Deadline passed (March 1, 2026). Add to calendar for next year. NYC residents only. Must attend a partner school or specific classes. Weekly in-person attendance required.

programs.name: PROMYS (Program in Mathematics for Young Scientists)
programs.desc: Elite residential mathematics program at Boston University focused on deep exploration of mathematical thinking. Students work on problem sets, number theory, and collaborate with Boston University math faculty. Alumni frequently go on to top math PhD programs. Highly recognized by admissions offices.
programs.note: Deadline passed for 2026. Add to calendar for next year.

programs.name: NYU ARISE
programs.org: New York University — Tandon School of Engineering
programs.desc: Free research program at NYU Tandon for NYC-area high schoolers. Students work in NYU labs on engineering and science research projects. Strong college application impact due to NYU affiliation and rigorous research experience.
programs.note: Deadline already passed for this year.

programs.name: Naval SEAP (Science & Engineering Apprenticeship Program)
programs.desc: Run by ASEE on behalf of the US Navy. Students are placed in active Naval Research Laboratory (NRL) or other DoD facilities doing real research alongside actual scientists. ~$3,500–4,500 stipend for the summer. One of the few HS programs with paid, authentic DoD lab research. Reddit consistently praises it as among the most 'real' research experiences available to high schoolers. Some students have published from SEAP placements.
programs.note: Deadline passed for 2026. Requires US citizenship. Apply next year.

programs.name: Columbia BrainYAC
programs.grades: High School Students (NYC area, underrepresented priority)
programs.desc: Students conduct neuroscience research in actual Columbia Zuckerman Institute labs — one of the world's premier brain science centers. Extremely small cohort (~15–25 students) makes this highly selective. Alumni have used BrainYAC research as the foundation for Regeneron STS and Siemens competition entries. Highly regarded by admissions officers familiar with it.
programs.note: Deadline passed for 2026. Add to calendar for next year. NYC-area students prioritized. Targets underrepresented students in neuroscience.

programs.name: George Mason ASSIP
programs.desc: Aspiring Scientists Summer Internship Program — one of the most popular and accessible free summer research programs in the US. Offers remote, hybrid, and in-person options. Essentially any STEM field available. Culminates in a poster presentation. Very broad research area coverage. $25 application fee.
programs.note: Deadline passed for 2026. Add to calendar for next year. $25 application fee (only cost).

programs.name: Columbia S-PREP
programs.desc: Columbia University's State Pre-College Enrichment Program for NY state residents. Provides underrepresented high schoolers with biomedical science exposure, mentorship, and college preparation. Columbia affiliation significantly boosts college application appeal.
programs.note: NY State residents only.

programs.name: UIUC Grainger Young Scholars STEMM Program
programs.grades: Rising 10th, 11th & 12th (IL, WI, IN, KY, MI, MO, IA)
programs.desc: Free STEM research program at UIUC focused on engineering applications across STEM fields. Residential housing available for non-local students. Strong program for students in the Midwest; UIUC's engineering reputation adds credibility.
programs.note: Limited to students in Wisconsin, Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Missouri, or Iowa.

programs.name: UIUC WYSE High School Summer STEM Research
programs.desc: Free faculty-mentored STEM research experience at UIUC for rising juniors and seniors. Non-residential. Culminates in a final poster symposium. Solid regional program with good university reputation behind it.
programs.note: Deadline passed for 2026. Add to calendar for next year.

programs.name: Library of Congress High School Summer Internship
programs.desc: Rare opportunity to intern at the largest library in the world. Students work with library collections, talk with visitors, and participate in museum research. Unique for humanities students; the Library of Congress name carries significant weight on college applications.
programs.note: Must be 16+. Hybrid program (some in-person in DC, some remote).

programs.name: Metropolitan Museum of Art — High School Internship
programs.grades: Juniors & Seniors (NYC metro: NY, CT, NJ)
programs.desc: Highly competitive and hands-on internship at one of the world's greatest art museums. Students receive a $1,100 stipend, conduct research, and present findings at the end. School-year internships also available. Outstanding for humanities or arts-focused students.
programs.note: Must live in NYC metro area (NY, CT, or NJ).

programs.name: NASA GL4HS (GeneLab for High School)
programs.desc: NASA's GeneLab for High School program exposes students to space biology and omics data analysis. Students analyze real NASA spaceflight data. The NASA name alone carries exceptional weight on college applications, regardless of program tier.

programs.name: Michigan State PAN Program
programs.org: Facility for Rare Isotope Beams (FRIB) — Michigan State
programs.desc: Free one-week residential program at Michigan State's nuclear physics facility. Students explore applied physics with a focus on nuclear physics, working alongside FRIB scientists. Short duration limits impact, but unique exposure to nuclear physics research.
programs.note: Deadline passed for 2026. Add to calendar for next year. Only 1 week long — less weight on applications compared to multi-week programs.

programs.name: UNH HighTech Bound
programs.org: University of New Hampshire — InterOperability Lab
programs.desc: Computer engineering internship at UNH's InterOperability Lab working with Fortune 500 companies. Less research-focused, more like a real engineering internship. Students earn high school credit and receive a stipend. No housing provided.
programs.note: No housing provided. Rising seniors only.

programs.name: King Conservation Science Scholars
programs.desc: Free research/internship opportunity at Chicago's Brookfield Zoo focused on animal and environmental sciences. Less paper-focused and more hands-on. Great for students interested in wildlife biology, conservation, or zoology. Career exploration focus.
programs.note: Chicago-area in-person program.

programs.name: Aresty Rutgers RISE
programs.desc: Rutgers' flagship undergraduate research office pairs high schoolers with faculty for lab research. Strong regional reputation in NJ/Northeast. Quality of experience varies by mentor placement. Best used as a launchpad for a publishable paper or science fair entry — impact depends on output produced. Some stipends available.
programs.note: Deadline passed for 2026. Add to calendar for next year.

programs.name: QCamp (Quantum Systems Accelerator)
programs.desc: DOE-backed quantum computing summer camp. Strong government science backing. Excellent for students interested in quantum information science.
programs.note: California only and dates already closed for this year.

programs.name: Casper College Quantum Science Camp
programs.desc: Free quantum science summer camp in Wyoming. Less prestigious than DOE/national-lab quantum programs, but free and accessible. Good introduction to quantum concepts for students who can travel to Wyoming.
programs.note: Located in Wyoming — travel required.

programs.name: Qubit by Qubit
programs.desc: Well-structured virtual quantum computing program with three tracks: Data Science (June 29–July 24), Quantum Computing (June 29–July 31), and AI (June 29–July 31). IBM and Google backed. Good intro to quantum for students with no prior experience.
programs.note: Dates conflict with India trip. Reconsider for next year if interested.

programs.name: Sci-MI (Science Mentorship Institute)
programs.desc: Free 501(c)(3) nonprofit pairing high school students with volunteer mentors for project-based summer research. Fields include neuroscience, biology, CS, electrical engineering, and chemistry. Good for students starting out in research with no prior experience.

programs.name: Sci-MI EEMP (Electrical Engineering Mentorship Program)
programs.desc: Free 5-week virtual electrical engineering research mentorship program. A significant part of the program is dedicated to writing a research paper that can be submitted for publication in student journals. Good for rising juniors/seniors interested in EE.

programs.name: SPARK SMP (Summer Mentorship Program)
programs.desc: Free program connecting students with companies and organizations doing research in the Seattle Metro Area. Covers STEM and some humanities/marketing projects. Favors Seattle-area students. Good for local Seattle students looking for industry exposure.
programs.note: Strongly favors students in the Seattle area.

programs.name: NECHR (Northeastern Centre for High School Research)
programs.desc: Nonprofit connecting high schoolers with university student mentors for free one-on-one and group research and entrepreneurship programs. Less well-known than other programs; requires registration to get full details.
programs.note: Must register on website to access program details.

programs.name: EnergyMag Virtual Internships
programs.selectivityLabel: Open / Very Low Barrier to Entry
programs.desc: Online energy publication offering virtual internship-style experiences (writing, content creation, research summaries on renewable energy). Very little presence in college admissions circles. More useful as a portfolio/writing samples builder for energy journalism or policy tracks than as a STEM research credential.
programs.note: Low admissions impact on its own. More of a content portfolio builder than a research credential.

programs.name: MiniPHD Program (JEI / Emerging Investigators)
programs.desc: Free 8-week program led by graduate/postdoc mentors producing a manuscript submitted to the Journal of Emerging Investigators (JEI) — a legitimate peer-reviewed journal for HS/undergrad researchers. A JEI publication is a citable, meaningful differentiator recognized by admissions officers.
programs.note: Targeted at 8th–10th graders. Key value: a published JEI paper is a real credential — pursue publication aggressively if admitted.

programs.name: Art MEDified C.R.A.F.T. Research Scholars
programs.desc: Free 8-week health literacy research camp where students conduct studies, learn R programming, navigate the Web of Science database, develop technical writing skills, and defend their work. Valuable technical skills (R, academic databases) but lower prestige.

programs.name: SRA RMP (Research Mentorship Program)
programs.desc: Matches high school students with university/industry mentors for independent research projects conducted remotely. The program name is not widely recognized by selective admissions offices — what matters is the output (paper, science fair entry). Good launchpad if you produce a publishable result.
programs.note: Deadline passed for 2026. Add to calendar for next year. Value comes from what you produce, not the program name.

programs.name: Boston Leadership Institute
programs.desc: For-profit, fee-based (~$3,000–5,000+) summer program with tracks in neuroscience, forensic science, biomedical research, etc. Admission is essentially guaranteed upon payment. College counselors on Reddit consistently flag BLI-type programs as 'resume padding' that admissions officers recognize and discount.
programs.note: CAUTION: Admissions officers at selective colleges are familiar with pay-to-attend programs. Invest the cost in authentic research opportunities instead.

programs.name: NJ Governor's School of Engineering & Technology
programs.deadline: Cannot apply — requires high school nomination
programs.desc: Prestigious NJ Governor's School program in engineering and technology at Rutgers. Highly regarded in NJ college admissions. Unfortunately requires a high school nomination — speak with your school counselor about being nominated for next year.
programs.note: Cannot self-apply — requires nomination from your high school. Ask your counselor about securing a nomination for next year.

programs.name: Waksman Student Scholars Program (WSSP / WISE)
programs.org: Waksman Institute of Microbiology — Rutgers University
programs.desc: Rutgers-based molecular biology and bioinformatics research program. Students purify DNA, perform PCR, restriction digests, gel electrophoresis, and use BLAST to analyze sequences — with discoveries published on GenBank (international DNA database) with student credit. Two tracks: WISE (2-week summer intensive) and WSSP (year-long, school/teacher-based).
programs.note: Closed for 2026 (positions filled as of March 16, 2026). Next application cycle opens ~February 1, 2027. Rolling admissions — apply early. In-person sessions at Piscataway, NJ.

programs.name: AMS Young Scholars Program (Epsilon Fund)
programs.desc: The AMS Epsilon Fund awards grants to directors of summer math programs for talented high schoolers. This grant supports elite programs like PROMYS, Ross, and similar intensive math camps — being admitted to an AMS-funded program is a strong college application credential. Note: applications are submitted by program directors, not students directly.
programs.note: This is a grant for math program directors, not a direct student application. To benefit, apply to programs that receive AMS Epsilon funding (e.g. PROMYS, Ross Mathematics Program). Next application cycle opens ~November 2026.
